title: Does the cyclic group C_1111 admit a (1111,186,31)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: The packet is already human-ready. Lean would provide a secondary archival seal by formalizing the multiplier normalization, contraction identity, and orbit contradiction, but it is not a prerequisite for publication readiness.
micro_paper_assessment: Pass. This is a genuine one-shot micro-paper packet: a short structural nonexistence theorem for a named Baumert-Gordon survivor row, with bounded remaining work limited to exposition and optional Lean sealing rather than a feeder ladder.
strongest_honest_claim: No cyclic (1111,186,31) difference set exists; a 5-multiplier orbit-and-contraction argument on Z_11 x Z_101 gives a direct contradiction, and the bounded publication audit did not uncover a prior settlement of this exact row.
family_affinity: Multiplier-plus-contraction obstruction for cyclic pq-order difference sets, with v = 11*101 and multiplier 5 coming from n = 155.
generalization_signal: The template plausibly extends to cyclic pq cases where a prime divisor of n yields a fixed-set normalization and the orbit lengths split into one short subgroup orbit and one long complement orbit.
paper_title_hint: On the Nonexistence of a Cyclic (1111,186,31) Difference Set
candidate_theorem_slice: Any 5-invariant subset D of Z_11 x Z_101 with the contracted data of a cyclic (1111,186,31) difference set forces the zero fiber count c_0 both into {6,21} and into 1 mod 25, impossible.
next_action: This packet is HUMAN_READY and has been moved to LEAN_QUEUE for formal sealing; do not block fresh curation/solve work on pending Lean.
